zh.lifeSummary: 當Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur於1335年出生在德國時，他的父親Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg51歲，母親Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin21歲。他和Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara至少有4個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1388年5月11日在德國尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 爾享年53歲，埋葬在德國薩辛-安哈爾特藍德可萊斯 維騰格維騰格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur于1335年出生在德国时，他的父亲Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg51岁，母亲Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin21岁。他和Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara至少有4个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1388年5月11日在德国尼德薩辛藍德可萊斯 爾享年53岁，埋葬在德国薩辛-安哈爾特藍德可萊斯 維騰格維騰格。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Herzog,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: zu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 祖, meaning ‘ancestors’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal names Zu Jia (祖甲), Zu Yi (祖乙), Zu Xin (祖辛), Zu Ding (祖丁), and Zu Geng (祖庚), personal names of several kings during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the personal names of Zu Ji (祖己) and Zu Yin (祖尹), who were descendants of an official called Zhong Hui who lived some time during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ).

Mandarin form of the surname 俎, meaning ‘sacrificial utensil’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by the Bao Zu (鮑俎) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (ii) said to be adopted by the fourth son of the uncle of the famous general Yue Fei (1103–1142  AD ), who changed the original surname Zhu (祝) to Zu (俎) in order to escape from political persecutions.

variants.name: Tsu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chen, Chi, Ching, Kin, Kung, Kuo, Yuk.  Vietnamese Mui, Loc.
Chinese:
alternative Mandarin forms of the Chinese surnames 祖 and 俎, see   Zu  .

nameMeanings.name: Sachsen,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: probably from a placename Sachenbach in southern Germany, the first part of which is from Old High German saher ‘reed grass’ or ‘swamp’.

nameMeanings.name: Herzog

nameMeanings.name: Wenceslas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of an East European Slavic name, composed of the elements ventie ‘more, greater’ + slav ‘glory’. St Wenceslas was a 10th-century duke of Bohemia noted for his piety, the grandson of St Ludmilla . He is regarded as the patron of Bohemia. This was also the name of four kings of Bohemia in the period covering the 13th to the 15th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur was born in 1335, in Germany, his father, Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg, was 51 and his mother, Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin, was 21. He had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara. He died on 11 May 1388, in Celle, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 53, and was buried in Wittenberg, Wittenberg, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur est né en 1335, à Allemagne, son père, Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles avec Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara. Il est décédé le 11 mai 1388, à Landkreis Celle, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur 1335 in Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg 51 und seine Mutter Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin 21. Er hatte mit Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 11. Mai 1388 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Landkreis Celle, Niedersachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur è nato il nel 1335 nel Germania, il suo padre, Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg, aveva 51 anni e il suo madre, Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie con Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara. Morì il 11 maggio 1388, nel Landkreis Celle, Niedersachsen, Germania, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lüneburが1335年にドイツで生まれた時，父Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenbergは51歳，母Agnes von Lindow-Ruppinは21歳であった。彼はCecilia Gigliola da Carraraとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1388年5月11日にドイツニーダーサチセンランドクレイス セルで53歳で死去し，ドイツサチセン-アンハルトランドクレイス ウィッテンバーグウィッテンバーグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur(이)가 1335년 독일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1388년 5월 11일 독일 니더작센 랜드크레이스 세에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 작센-아할트 랜드크레이스 윗텐버그 윗텐버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur nasceu em 1335, em Alemanha, seu pai, Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas com Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara. Ele faleceu em 11 de maio de 1388, em Landkreis Celle, Niedersachsen, Alemanha, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur родился в 1335 г. в местности Германия, его отцу по имени Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin, 21. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara. Он умер 11 мая 1388 г. в местности ландкрис селл, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности виттенбирг, ландкрис виттенбирг, сачсен-анэлт, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Wenceslas von Sachsen-Wittenberg, I, Herzog, Kurfürst zu Sachsen, Herzog zu Braunschweig-Lünebur nació en 1335, en Alemania, su papá, Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Agnes von Lindow-Ruppin, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas con Cecilia Gigliola da Carrara. Murió el 11 de mayo de 1388, en Landkreis Celle, Niedersachsen, Alemania, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemania.
